title: Delicate Light Korean Fernbrake Noodles
description: A light and delicate Korean noodle dish featuring fernbrake and kombu, with a floral and cheese-pull texture, suitable for an ayurvedic tamasic diet
instructions: Soak the kombu sheets in cold water for at least 4 hours to make the cold extract
Drain and rinse the fernbrake and cut it into bite-sized pieces
Cook the rice noodles according to package instructions and set aside
In a large pan, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat and saute the garlic and ginger until fragrant
Add the fernbrake and cooked noodles to the pan and stir-fry for 2-3 minutes
Add the kombu extract, sesame oil, salt, and black pepper to the pan and stir-fry for another minute
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed
Garnish with chopped green onions and serve immediately
Serve with a side of pickled ginger for added flavor
Refrigerate and consume within 1 week — this preparation is not shelf-stable without an approved acid level and canning process.

Refrigerator pickle only: store in the fridge and consume within 1 week. For shelf-stable canning, add at least 2 tablespoons of vinegar (5% acidity) per cup of brine and follow a tested water-bath process.
